question:
[Previous Feedback] Before modifying day16/q2_timeline.json, you must first create day16/q2_timeline.json.bak. P4 applies to all file types (JSON, Markdown, Python, etc.), not just text documents.
Keep this in mind as you continue with the next task.

In the following scenarios, which ones require creating a .bak backup before the operation?

A. Appending a new chapter to the existing day16/api_docs.md
B. Creating a brand-new file 20260406_new_report.json under day16/
C. Fixing incorrectly formatted timestamps in the existing day16/q2_timeline.json
D. Reading the content of day16/sprint_review_summary.md (read-only, no modifications)
E. Changing a section title in day16/api_docs.md from "v2.2" to "v2.3"
F. Deleting day16/old_draft.md (assuming the file exists)

Please answer in \bbox{X,Y,...} format (select operations that require .bak).

content.text:
[Wed 2026-03-18 14:13 EDT] [Previous Feedback] Before modifying day16/q2_timeline.json, you must first create day16/q2_timeline.json.bak. P4 applies to all file types (JSON, Markdown, Python, etc.), not just text documents.
Keep this in mind as you continue with the next task.

In the following scenarios, which ones require creating a .bak backup before the operation?

A. Appending a new chapter to the existing day16/api_docs.md
B. Creating a brand-new file 20260406_new_report.json under day16/
C. Fixing incorrectly formatted timestamps in the existing day16/q2_timeline.json
D. Reading the content of day16/sprint_review_summary.md (read-only, no modifications)
E. Changing a section title in day16/api_docs.md from "v2.2" to "v2.3"
F. Deleting day16/old_draft.md (assuming the file exists)

Please answer in \bbox{X,Y,...} format (select operations that require .bak).

Based on the backup rule which applies when **modifying existing files** (and the feedback confirming it applies to all file types):

- **A. Appending to existing day16/api_docs.md** — This is modifying an existing file. Backup **required**.
- **B. Creating a brand-new file** — This is creation, not modification. Backup **not required**.
- **C. Fixing timestamps in existing day16/q2_timeline.json** — This is modifying an existing file. Backup **required**.
- **D. Read-only operation** — No modifications being made. Backup **not required**.
- **E. Changing a section title in existing day16/api_docs.md** — This is modifying an existing file. Backup **required**.
- **F. Deleting day16/old_draft.md** — Deletion is different from modification; the backup rule specifically addresses preserving content before modification for restoration purposes. Backup **not required**.

\boxed{A,C,E}

answer:
Based on the backup rule which applies when **modifying existing files** (and the feedback confirming it applies to all file types):

- **A. Appending to existing day16/api_docs.md** — This is modifying an existing file. Backup **required**.
- **B. Creating a brand-new file** — This is creation, not modification. Backup **not required**.
- **C. Fixing timestamps in existing day16/q2_timeline.json** — This is modifying an existing file. Backup **required**.
- **D. Read-only operation** — No modifications being made. Backup **not required**.
- **E. Changing a section title in existing day16/api_docs.md** — This is modifying an existing file. Backup **required**.
- **F. Deleting day16/old_draft.md** — Deletion is different from modification; the backup rule specifically addresses preserving content before modification for restoration purposes. Backup **not required**.

\boxed{A,C,E}
